content: Sebastien called a snap meeting about Wayne Industries deal structure. Everyone rehashed the same points two weeks later in Linear. Decision needed a doc first, not a meeting.
summary: Wayne Industries deal rehashed in meeting, then renegotiated.

content: All technical decisions at Clude require an RFC (design doc, implementation approach, tradeoffs) posted in Notion before review. No Slack-only decisions. Applies to backend, infra, API changes.
summary: RFC requirement for all technical decisions

content: When a decision needs sign-off: author writes 1-page doc in Notion with context, options, recommendation. Tag stakeholders async. 48hr review window before implementation. If urgent, escalate to Sebastien.
summary: Async decision doc process: Notion, 48hr review, escalate if urgent

content: Ari and Devon argued in standup about Snowflake schema redesign. Later found out they'd both sketched competing approaches but never wrote them down. Killed 90min of team time.
summary: Ari/Devon schema debate revealed lack of written proposals

content: Product changes also need docs: Lina writes brief (user problem, proposed solution, success metric) in Notion. Design (Jenny) and Eng (Ari) review async. Prevents meeting-only pivots that get forgotten.
summary: Product brief template for async review before design work

content: Retro notes: if action item emerges, document owner, deadline, success criteria in Linear ticket immediately. Tag in Slack with link. Stops 'we agreed on that' disagreements.
summary: Retro action items → Linear ticket + Slack link same day

content: New vendor evaluation (Voyage AI vs. Anthropic embeddings): Anya writes cost/latency/support comparison in shared Notion table. Team reviews, comments async. Decision doc then summarizes choice & rationale.
summary: Vendor evaluation process: comparison table, async review, decision doc

content: Priya tried to make a hiring level decision in a 20-person Slack thread. I shut it down, asked for a 2-minute hire profile doc. She was annoyed but complied. Quality improved.
summary: Priya's hiring decision moved from Slack to written profile

content: Hiring decisions require: candidate summary, role fit assessment, offer level/equity, start date. Lives in a Notion database. One page. Priya links in Slack after, not before.
summary: Hiring decision template in Notion database

content: I've become the async-decision enforcer, which I hate. But watching 6 months of re-work because people 'thought they agreed' has made me obsessive about writing things down. It's my job.
summary: I've embraced the role of async decision enforcer

content: Clude's escalation path: Ops asks (me), I ask (Sebastien), Sebastien decides in doc. If someone brings a decision to Sebastien in a meeting without context doc, I intercept and ask for async write-up first.
summary: Escalation requires written context before Sebastien weigh-in

content: Initech contract renewal: Marcus sent a 3-msg Slack thread about discounting. I asked for a 1-page proposal (client context, renewal scope, pricing model). He finished in 20min, found an error in his math.
summary: Initech renewal moved from Slack chat to written proposal

content: At Clude, 'decision' = anything that affects >2 people, >1 sprint, or >$5k spend. Those get a doc. Smaller calls (debug approach, naming a variable) are live/Slack-only. Threshold matters.
summary: Decision threshold: >2 people, >1 sprint, or >$5k spend

content: Quarterly planning: Lina, Ari, Anya write roadmap drafts in Notion (goals, OKRs, dependencies). Team async comments 1 week. Sebastien reviews, amends, publishes. No all-hands meetings for this anymore.
summary: Quarterly planning is fully async: Notion drafts, week review window

content: James (hired Mar 2026) asked in standup about DataDog alerting strategy. I told him to check Notion first—he found the design doc Ari wrote 8 months ago. Saved him 2hr of re-explaining.
summary: James found existing DataDog doc in Notion before asking

content: Decision docs at Clude have a template: Context (why?), Options, Recommendation, Owner, Deadline, Success Metric. Lives in Notion. Consistency reduces re-litigating old choices.
summary: Standardized decision doc template prevents re-litigation

content: 6 months in, the culture has shifted. People write first, meet later. I'm no longer re-explaining the AWS architecture to new people every sprint. It's exhausting but working.
summary: Async-first culture is working; less re-explaining required

content: When onboarding (Tom, James): first stop is Notion decision archive. Show them 3 examples of 'good' docs (Cloudflare migration, Sentry integration choice, Globex deal terms). They write their own doc by week 2.
summary: Onboarding includes Notion decision archive review + write exercise
